objective: Determine whether Chavez 83.8% on Riemann zero gaps reflects gap ordering, gap distribution, or both; test scale stability

parameters.note: Synthetic control used mean=1.41 (full 999-gap mean), not 2.246 (actual 99-gap mean) — distribution mismatch; phase 3 will correct

shuffle_test.verdict: Ordering does NOT matter — mean delta +0.1 pts, well below 5-pt threshold

synthetic_exponential_gaps.note: Mean mismatch: actual 99-gap mean is 2.246; this control draws from a different regime
synthetic_exponential_gaps.verdict: Borderline — 3.7 pts below baseline, sub-threshold (threshold=5). Confounded by mean mismatch.

scale_invariance.verdict: CONFIRMED scale-stable — symmetry rises slightly with n, reaching 86.2% at n=249

scale_invariant.verdict: Confirmed — signal grows slightly with n

F1_ordering_irrelevant.significance: Chavez conjugation symmetry is ordering-invariant at this n. The 83.8% is a property of the gap SIZE DISTRIBUTION, not the sequential arrangement. Cannot detect GUE long-range pair correlations (which are ordering-dependent).

F2_exponential_borderline.answer: Borderline separation (3.7 pts, sub-threshold), confounded by mean mismatch
F2_exponential_borderline.significance: Actual 99-gap mean is 2.246, not 1.41. Phase 3 must re-run with mean=2.246 (clean test) and Wigner surmise GUE gaps for a true Poisson vs. GUE comparison.

F3_scale_stable.answer: Scale stable — symmetry holds and rises slightly to 86.2% at n=249
F3_scale_stable.significance: Strongest result from Phase 2b. The 83.8% is not a small-n artifact. At n=249 the zeros approach Sophie Germain gap territory (90.1%).

phase3_priorities: Mean-matched synthetic control: Exponential(mean=2.246), n=99, seeds 1-3
Wigner surmise GUE gaps: P(s) proportional to s*exp(-pi*s^2/4), n=99, compare to Poisson baseline
AT-1 annihilation topology: Biss-Dugger-Isaksen literature check, then classify all 84 zero divisor pairs
